title: Question on Gods existence in Evangelion
display_title: Question on Gods existence in Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Question on God's existence in Evangelion             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Thu Jan 29, 2009 5:48 am Does God exist in Evangelion? I'm not counting that he exists in people's minds, I'm talking about an actual presence, because there are churches in Evangelion ( episode 16), Misato has a cross necklace, and there is a lot of religious symbolism throughout the show, but does God really exist in Evangelion? It doesn't seem like He would have a role, so maybe he existed but fell from prominence, or...?? Evangelion is Existentialist, but that just represents the people's beliefs in the show, it has nothing to do with actual presence. Does this mean that the role of being God is open ended, does the capability to become God only exist without God existing? It's true that there may be no answer to this question, but I just don't see how Shinji can claim the power of God and how man can be dirtied by original sin if God doesn't exist. To explain God's presence isn't part of the core of Evangelion, so I should add a disclaimer saying that this might not qualify as 'Evangelion Discussion' material because answers could be based just on complete speculation.

[]: Re: Question on God's existence in Evangelion             Quote Post by Another Asuka » Thu Jan 29, 2009 5:56 am Oedipusfoot wrote: but I just don't see how Shinji can claim the power of God and how man can be dirtied by original sin if God doesn't exist.

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Thu Jan 29, 2009 6:22 am I thought that someone would come out with a strange answer justifying God's existence, so I didn't think about that. Though, this is probably something no one feels like talking about.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Thu Jan 29, 2009 6:32 am Either God is The First Ancestral Race or all the Evas... Oh the controversy!

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Thu Jan 29, 2009 7:04 am Religion certainly does exist in Evangelion, but the existence of God (and I'm assuming you mean a christian God) is irrelevant. He doesn't feature in the story line therefore he may aswell not exist. Now if your talking about a more loose term of God, than Adam or Lilith could be considered gods, as they give life, as could the FAR. Quoting from the CI: "Within a carrier known as a "Moon", the First Ancestral Race fabricated a perfect cavity (also a moon), at which point the Seed, or "Progenitor Entity", would be placed inside and sent out into space. That was their technology, and, from the perspective of Angels, humans, and others, they might be called gods."

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Thu Jan 29, 2009 7:31 am "CI"?

[]:             Quote Post by majlund » Thu Jan 29, 2009 7:41 am Oedipusfoot wrote: "CI"?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Jan 29, 2009 8:53 am The parenthesis in the heading are breaking the link. No need to worry, though, because the redirect sends you to the right page anyway. It's when linking to a particular section that you'll have a problem. (We might have to rename the article headings to get rid of the parenthesis)

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Jan 29, 2009 9:25 am phpbb is just retarded when it parses links, you just need to encode the parens using % http://www.evageeks.org/FGP/Classified_Information_%28Translation%29

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Thu Jan 29, 2009 10:34 am This might provide some more answers for you. For me, a god isn't necessary in Eva so, for all intents and purposes, there isn't one. While there are many existential elements to the story, none are of the religious variety.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Thu Jan 29, 2009 12:48 pm God, a being always watching over its children I guess would be Lilith to the Lilim and Adam to the Angels. Now Shinji has the ability to become a supreme God but chooses to return to Earth and have everyone else return if they will it so.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Thu Jan 29, 2009 1:00 pm OP: Define "God" as opposed to "god". How would you determine his/her/its existence, apart from the overt representations of belief? Even with those references, when are characters referring to a (pressumed Judeo-Christian) deity as opposed to "a godlike creation"?

[]:             Quote Post by Annihilationscape » Thu Jan 29, 2009 1:16 pm Agreed. Additional clarification would help us further the discussion. As it stands, however, I've accepted that, for the purposes of this fictional universe, there is no "God" in the Judeo-Christian sense. Unless EoE is a totally unique interpretation of Revelation. ...Actually that's kinda cool, thinking about it :P

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Thu Jan 29, 2009 3:02 pm I meant God, otherwise there would be no capital there, and because of the nature of Evangelion's symbolism. But after reading some of the 'CI' information, I could change that even though it probably won't go very far here and it wasn't the point of the thread. That question has died, so this is all over anyway. A deviation! The 'CI' section had a lot of juicy information. 'CI' spoke of the First Ancestral Race, then said that they could be considered gods. Was that Ps2 game the first place this was mentioned? Can it be believed that some alien race allowed the Angels to form? Why would they do that? How would that connect with whatever prophesy was in the Dead Sea Scrolls? Why does SEELE have the Dead Sea Scrolls (what made them entitled to them beyond what their plan is)? Don't kill me if this is somewhere else. The First Ancestral Race reminds me of the 'Adams' in 2.0.

[]:             Quote Post by Another Asuka » Thu Jan 29, 2009 3:08 pm Theist: But you can't disprove the existence of God! Atheist: Nor can you disprove that the Events of Eva really happened in an alternative Universe and Shinji, who is now God, is using Anno as his prohet to warn us the world will end unless we interprete the end of Eva as Shinji(God) intended.

[]: Re: Question on God's existence in Evangelion             Quote Post by slothen » Thu Jan 29, 2009 3:50 pm Oedipusfoot wrote: I just don't see how Shinji can claim the power of God and how man can be dirtied by original sin if God doesn't exist.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Jan 29, 2009 3:52 pm Does God exist in Evangelion? I'm not counting that he exists in people's minds, I'm talking about an actual presence, because there are churches in Evangelion ( episode 16), Misato has a cross necklace, and there is a lot of religious symbolism throughout the show, but does God really exist in Evangelion? It doesn't seem like He would have a role, so maybe he existed but fell from prominence, or...??

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Thu Jan 29, 2009 3:56 pm AuraTwilight wrote: SEELE found them the same way they found Adam and the Lance, I'd assume.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Thu Jan 29, 2009 4:07 pm Oedipusfoot wrote: I meant God

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jan 29, 2009 4:55 pm VoidEater wrote: Definition, please. And how to determine existence of said. Really, now, you might as well say that since there is a "creation" there must be "God" if you insist on this level of dialogue.
